pi_items.source: setup_task.sh: postgres service (line 98) has container_name postgres-db; main.py (line 22) expects host db.inventory.local on port 5432 (PostgreSQL default)
setup_task.sh: redis service (line 104) has container_name redis-cache; main.py (line 23) expects host cache.inventory.local on port 6379 (Redis default)
setup_task.sh: mock-auth service (line 108) has container_name auth-service; main.py (line 24) expects host auth.provider.external on port 80
setup_task.sh main.py (line 63) prints this exact message; verifier.py (line 77) checks for this exact string

privileged_info_summary: The task creates a Docker-based legacy application at /home/ga/legacy-migration with a container named legacy-app. The app requires three network aliases to function: postgres-db service must be aliased as db.inventory.local (PostgreSQL port 5432), redis-cache service must be aliased as cache.inventory.local (Redis port 6379), and auth-service must be aliased as auth.provider.external (HTTP port 80). The application logs the success message 'All systems go! Application started.' when all three services are reachable. The task uses official Docker images (postgres:16-alpine, redis:alpine, nginx:alpine) and all metadata values have been verified against the actual task implementation.
